changes_made: Reduced skill from 573 lines (verbose generic content) to ~100 lines of focused, actionable patterns
Added explicit instruction to IGNORE LSP diagnostics and just run scripts
Replaced defaultdict patterns with plain dict.get() to avoid Pyright type errors that cause infinite edit loops
Replaced max(dict, key=dict.get) with sorted(..., key=lambda)[0][0] for type-safety
Provided complete copy-pasteable Python script patterns for data cleaning with correct date handling logic
Added instruction to write NEW script files on error instead of editing (avoids infinite edit loops)
Added rule against f-string + concatenation across lines (prevents SyntaxError)
Changed dates_standardized counting to use deduplicated rows only (matches test expectations)
Simplified multi-dataset report pattern to avoid complex string concatenation
key_findings: devstral-small frequently degenerates into infinite loops: either repeating output tokens or retrying identical edits
LSP/Pyright diagnostics in opencode trigger the model to attempt fixes, often getting stuck in edit loops with identical old/new strings
The model cannot reliably fix its own syntax errors - it reproduces the same bug when rewriting
Shorter, more prescriptive skills with complete code patterns work better than abstract guidance
The multi-dataset-report task is unstable: the model consistently introduces a trailing dot in string concatenation that it cannot self-correct
